title: Startup import-chain hang — supabase eager import via tools_memory
what_failed: First chat run terminated with KeyboardInterrupt deep inside tenacity -> pyiceberg -> storage3 -> supabase import chain. Second run worked because the import was cached. Supabase alone takes ~1.2s to import on cold disk.
where_failed: tools/tools_memory.py: `from supabase import create_client` at module top. This causes every `import pi_agent` to pay the ~1.2s supabase import cost even before any MemoryTools instance is created.
why_likely: Supabase transitively imports pyiceberg which has a slow cold-disk import chain. The fix is lazy import — only pay the cost when MemoryTools is instantiated.
reproduction: time python -c 'import pi_agent' — observe ~1.8s on cold disk. The supabase import alone is ~1.2s of that.
expected: `import pi_agent` does not trigger the supabase import chain. MemoryTools instantiation pays the cost (still at startup, but isolated and isolatable).
actual: Any `import pi_agent` triggered ~1.2s supabase import via module-level `from supabase import create_client`.
suggested_fix: Move `from supabase import create_client` from module top to inside `MemoryTools.__init__`.
fix_summary: Moved `from supabase import create_client` from module top into MemoryTools.__init__. Updated three test helpers (test_t026_dedup_and_profile.py, test_memory_tools_gaps.py, test_inferred_facts.py) to remove the now-unnecessary `patch('tools.tools_memory.create_client')` context managers — all three use MemoryTools.__new__ which bypasses __init__, so the patch was a no-op safety net. `import pi_agent` time dropped from 1.82s to 1.21s (−0.61s, ~34%).

verification.result: 2 tests passed (subprocess isolation): importing tools.tools_memory and pi_agent both show 'supabase' NOT in sys.modules after module import
